meta.book: Lay, Linear Algebra and Its Applications

preambles.text: In parts (a)–(f), v1, …, vp are vectors in a nonzero finite-dimensional vector space V, and S = {v1, …, vp}.

items.label: 1a
items.text: The set of all linear combinations of v1,…,vp is a vector space.

items.label: 1b
items.text: If {v1,…,vp−1} spans V, then S spans V.

items.label: 1c
items.text: If {v1,…,vp−1} is linearly independent, then so is S.

items.label: 1d
items.text: If S is linearly independent, then S is a basis for V.

items.label: 1e
items.text: If Span S = V, then some subset of S is a basis for V.

items.label: 1f
items.text: If dim V = p and Span S = V, then S cannot be linearly dependent.

items.label: 1g
items.text: A plane in R^3 is a two-dimensional subspace.

items.label: 1h
items.text: The nonpivot columns of a matrix are always linearly dependent.

items.label: 1i
items.text: Row operations on a matrix A can change the linear dependence relations among the rows of A.

items.label: 1j
items.text: Row operations on a matrix can change the null space.

items.label: 1k
items.text: The rank of a matrix equals the number of nonzero rows.

items.label: 1l
items.text: If an m×n matrix A is row equivalent to an echelon matrix U and if U has k nonzero rows, then the dimension of the solution space of Ax = 0 is m − k.

items.label: 1m
items.text: If B is obtained from a matrix A by several elementary row operations, then rank B = rank A.

items.label: 1n
items.text: The nonzero rows of a matrix A form a basis for Row A.

items.label: 1o
items.text: If matrices A and B have the same reduced echelon form, then Row A = Row B.

items.label: 1p
items.text: If H is a subspace of R^3, then there is a 3×3 matrix A such that H = Col A.

items.label: 1q
items.text: If A is m×n and rank A = m, then the linear transformation x ↦ Ax is one-to-one.

items.label: 1r
items.text: If A is m×n and the linear transformation x ↦ Ax is onto, then rank A = m.

items.label: 1s
items.text: A change-of-coordinates matrix is always invertible.

items.label: 1t
items.text: If B = {b1,…,bn} and C = {c1,…,cn} are bases for a vector space V, then the jth column of the change-of-coordinates matrix PC←B is the coordinate vector [cj]B.
